stories.headline: Fed minutes signal split on timing of next rate move
stories.summary: Minutes from the May FOMC meeting showed members 'broadly agreed' inflation progress had slowed but disagreed on when to next adjust the policy rate. Traders moved expectations to September.

perspectives.keyFraming: Centers on what the minutes actually said and how rates markets repriced.
perspectives.summary: Wire and market-focused coverage notes that fed funds futures shifted to imply a September cut at roughly 55% probability, up from 40% before the release. Several outlets quote economists saying the minutes 'lean dovish on the margin.'

sources.title: Fed minutes show divided committee on rate path
Traders bring forward Fed cut bets after minutes release

perspectives.keyFraming: Frames the minutes as evidence the Fed is behind the curve on inflation persistence.
perspectives.summary: Right-leaning financial coverage emphasizes commentary from members concerned that services inflation remains sticky, and argues that any near-term cut would be premature given housing and wage data.

sources.title: Fed minutes spotlight sticky services inflation

perspectives.keyFraming: Official Fed communication preserves optionality and emphasizes data dependence.
perspectives.summary: The Federal Reserve's published minutes reiterate the committee's commitment to its 2% inflation target and note continued data dependence. No forward guidance on timing of the next move was offered.

sources.title: Minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee, May 2026

stories.headline: Brent slips below $82 as OPEC+ signals possible output increase
stories.summary: Brent crude fell more than 2% intraday after delegates indicated a working group is studying a modest output increase for Q3. WTI followed, dipping below $78.

perspectives.keyFraming: Wire reporting treats the slip as a supply-driven reaction to OPEC+ signaling.
perspectives.summary: Mainstream wires note Brent traded as low as $81.40 in London hours after delegates told reporters a working group is studying production increases. Analysts surveyed expect no formal decision before the June meeting.

sources.title: Brent slips as OPEC+ delegates discuss possible Q3 output rise
Oil drops as OPEC+ weighs adding barrels
